_comment: Generated by macro-analyst on 2026-04-29 (pre-open, Bucharest 07:36 EEST). Coalition crisis escalates: PSD+AUR motion of no-confidence formally FILED Apr 28 with 251 signatures (>233 threshold) — vote expected within ~5 sessions. EUR/RON Apr 28 BNR 5.0937 (still above 5.05 band, streak 36 days). RO 10Y rose to 7.32% on Apr 27 (+11bp d/d) — still under 7.40% trigger. DAX -0.19% Apr 28 (modestly negative); VIX 17.83 (-5.77%); Brent rallied +2.71% to 111.16 on Mid-East premium. TTF 43.59 (-2.83% wk) — below €50 threshold. WIG20 -0.76%. NBR meets May 7 (8 days) — first cut consensus but unconfirmed. All 3 agencies BBB-/Baa3 Negative; S&P affirmed BBB-/Neg Apr 3 (latest action); no new agency action queued. BET Apr 27 close 29,172 (-0.40%) — sitting ~1.5% below ATH. Source feeds: BNR XML (FX), Yahoo (^GDAXI, ^VIX, BZ=F-via-WebSearch, TTF=F, EURRON=X, WIG20.WA), tradingeconomics (RO 10Y, BET, Brent), WebSearch (PNRR/coalition/ratings). Yahoo cache returned stale data for DAX/Brent — substituted live values from tradingeconomics WebSearch.

_qualitative_context.note: Day 9 of coalition crisis: PSD+AUR motion of no-confidence was formally FILED on Apr 28 with 251 signatures (vs 233 threshold). Vote expected within ~5 sessions. Bolojan refusing resignation; Nicusor Dan continues consultations. PNRR EUR 10bn at risk by August deadline; 9 reform laws (public-sector wages, fiscal, energy) pending; 57% of remaining critical reforms under PSD-coordinated ministries. BET prior session: -0.40% to 29,172 on Apr 27 (last data point). Brent +2.71% to USD 111.16 on Mid-East tension — net SNP tailwind (+ COM-1 marginal trigger). TTF 43.59 €/MWh — below €50 (COM-2 not armed). EUR/RON 5.0937 — still above 5.05 band 36 sessions, but no break of 5.10 (FX-3 not fired). RO 10Y 7.32% — below 7.40% trigger but +11bp on Apr 27 alone, +19bp over 10 days; momentum negative. DAX -0.19% gentle, VIX 17.83 dropping — global risk tone benign, contrast with active idiosyncratic Romanian risk. NBR May 7 meeting in 8 days — first cut consensus but unconfirmed by NBR communication (RAT-3 not yet armed; arms 2 days pre-meeting). MSCI June 2026 review still on calendar. Active themes: NBR pivot (slightly headwind today on no-confidence noise pre-meeting); MSCI upgrade June review (intact); Rating tail (warning level — coalition fall raises downgrade probability sharply, watch agency-action queue and CDS); Post-election relief (degraded/late-stage on coalition collapse). Regime caveat in force: 70% YoY rally to ATH biases mean-reversion; lean defensive, skeptical of breakouts.
_qualitative_context.regime_caveat: 70% YoY rally to ATH still active per reference doc — bias mean-reversion, skeptical of breakouts, weight relief-rally triggers.
